brief_document.title: Shared EventStore behavioural test suite
brief_document.purpose: Provide the reusable behavioural conformance suite that defines what a correct EventStore does. Durable backends (libSQL, Postgres) run this same suite as their correctness oracle.
brief_document.task: Build a reusable test suite, parameterised over a factory that produces a fresh EventStore, that exercises the full contract: append ordering, atomicity, the sequence-conflict guard, read_history, list_active, query filtering, and timer scheduling/expiry. Run it against InMemoryStore. Export it so later store clusters can run it against their backends.

requirements.title: Reusable conformance suite
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a behavioural test suite parameterised over an async factory closure returning a fresh Arc<dyn EventStore>, exercising every method of the contract and its documented edge cases.
acceptance: A module (exported behind a test-support feature or as a pub test-utility) defines run_event_store_suite(make_store) covering: append+read_history round-trip preserving order; multi-batch append with advancing sequence; SequenceConflict on stale expected_seq with no partial write; list_active reflecting projected status before and after a terminal event; query across type/status/time-range filters; schedule_timer + expired_timers boundary (due vs not-yet-due); timer re-schedule idempotency.
Each scenario asserts the documented contract, not implementation details, so any conformant backend passes.
The suite is reusable from another crate (visibility and feature gating allow AS and AX to call it).

requirements.title: Run the suite against InMemoryStore
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL run the conformance suite against InMemoryStore and the suite SHALL pass.
acceptance: An integration test invokes run_event_store_suite with a factory that yields a fresh InMemoryStore.
The test passes under cargo test.
The test is a real runtime test, not gated out or ignored.

boundaries: SHALL NOT test durability across process restarts — that is exercised by durable backends (AS/AX) using this suite plus their own persistence tests.
SHALL NOT bake in InMemoryStore-specific assumptions; the suite must pass for any conformant backend.
verification: cargo test -p aion-store passes, including the conformance run.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: A reusable behavioural test suite exercises append/read/list/query/timers plus conflict and atomicity, runnable against any EventStore, and passes against InMemoryStore.

stories.text: As a store author, I want an in-memory reference implementation and a shared behavioural test suite so that I can verify my backend matches the contract exactly.

constraints.text: lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion-core and aion-store are leaf crates — they depend on no other Aion crate. Structural; must hold.
Every type that crosses the wire or the store derives Serialize + Deserialize (these are data types, not credentials).
WorkflowStatus is always derivable from event history; no independent status mutation.
InMemoryStore must pass the same behavioural test suite future durable backends are held to — it is the oracle, so it must be correct.
Identifiers are newtypes; no bare Uuid or String in public signatures where an ID is meant.

resolved_context.intention: Define the vocabulary every other part of Aion speaks: the event types, identifiers, workflow status, error taxonomy, and the persistence contract. When the engine records an activity completion, a client lists running workflows, a store persists history, or a worker reports a result, they all use the types defined here. The types must make illegal states unrepresentable — a completed workflow cannot also be running, an activity result cannot exist without a preceding schedule. Nothing here depends on beamr, the engine, networking, or any storage backend. This is the still point at the centre: pure types and one trait, so the rest of Aion can be built against stable contracts.
